financial_signal: Overall FY26E Revenue: ~₹36 crs, EBITDA: ~8%, PAT: 6-7% | Consumer Products (Eha Brand) Revenue: ~ ₹24-26 crs | Sale of raw sustainable materials Revenue : ~₹10–12 crs | EBITDA FY25: ~6% | 7 cr
comments:
- Company Overview : EHA is a sustainability-led materials and consumer products company focused on developing eco-conscious alternatives across planters, packaging, and building materials

Founder Background: 
- Founder: Mahadev Chikkanna
- Experience: ~25 years
- First ~15 years in the IT industry (Infosys, L&T Tech), working across the US and Europe

Eha Journey:
- In 2015, driven by a personal calling toward social entrepreneurship and climate impact, decided to start his own venture
- 2016–2019 dedicated largely to R&D - Focused on developing sustainable materials, initially targeted at automotive applications
- First commercial order in 2019 enabled the setup of EHA’s first manufacturing facility
- Pivot During COVID (2020) : Began selling face shields, listing them on Amazon
- Post-COVID Growth & Brand Formation : Continued selling on Amazon post-COVID and gradually expanded the product portfolio

Business Verticals & Brands
- Consumer Products (EHA brand) : Planters and home/lifestyle products
- Packaging Materials : Brand: Lowpak
- Supplies sustainable packaging solutions : Clients include brands like MAE, Sleepy Owl, Araku Coffee, etc., who use EHA’s materials
- Building Materials (3S vertical) : Tiles, wall cladding, flooring solutions. Not actively pushed yet, but seen as a future growth lever

Channel Expansion for Consumer Products
- Scaled Amazon presence for ~1.5 years
- Expanded into: B2B sales to corporates and later Quick commerce & marketplaces (Zepto, Flipkart, etc.)

Product & Category Strategy
- Initial entry price point was ~₹600
- Gradually expanded into higher ASP products (₹1,500–2,000), which are now best sellers
- Market share of ~3–4% in planters on Amazon
- Recently launched landscape planters

Materials & Sustainability
- Core strength lies in material innovation
- Raw materials sourced include: Crop waste, Bamboo waste and Wood waste
- These are processed at the Mysore plant and converted into fibres
- Fibres are combined using binders made from recycled or bio-waste plastics

Manufacturing & Infrastructure
- EHA manufactures its materials in-house
- Manufacturing facility located in Mysore
- Experience centre in Bangalore (towards Mysore)

Financial Snapshot
- Overall FY26E Revenue: ~₹36 crs, EBITDA: ~8%, PAT: 6-7% 
- Consumer Products (Eha Brand) Revenue: ~ ₹24-26 crs
- Sale of raw sustainable materials Revenue : ~₹10–12 crs 
- EBITDA FY25: ~6%
- Asset-light business with minimal depreciation and interest costs
- Inventory levels: ~₹3.6–3.7 cr
- Amazon and other e-commerce channels each contribute ~₹70–80 lakh
